payload.answer: Store every row as a time-stamped, immutable entry with a composite key (id, timestamp). Normalize by creating a TableRow entity per record; keep historical versions via timestamp and version fields. Use an append-only log strategy with tombstone markers for deletions to preserve history. De-duplication should be applied only when a record with identical id and timestamp is encountered; otherwise, treat as distinct events.
payload.decision: Store the CSV-derived records in a structured, query-friendly memory schema with explicit versioning and de-duplication. Preserve ambiguity of repeated rows by treating identical id/name/value triples as potentially distinct temporal entries unless proven same record at same timestamp. Use immutable, append-only storage with per-row metadata to support auditing and conflict resolution.

entity_resolution.reason: Ambiguity arises when identical id/name/value triples occur with different timestamps or sources. Resolution defers until timestamped context is available.

memory_cards.statement: Define a normalized, time-aware memory model: TableRow(id, name, value, timestamp, source, version). Enforce a composite key (id, timestamp) for uniqueness; allow multiple rows for same id if timestamp differs to reflect updates over time.
Append-only log of rows with tombstones for deletions. Maintain a separate DeletionRow(exists, id, timestamp) to mark removals without erasing history.

details: Memory schema proposed: TableRow with timestamp/version, append-only storage, tombstones for deletions.
Ambiguity preserved with temporal keys; no facts superseded.

repair_options: Add timestamp column to CSV ingestion and generate incremental version numbers.
Implement unique constraint on (id, timestamp) for the in-memory store; allow duplicates only with different timestamps.
Introduce a tombstone row when a delete is observed to preserve history.
Maintain provenance: source file identifier and ingestion time for each row.
